rows.summary: Active child current_repo_head bindings were stale and are now rebound to the actual git HEAD without rewriting historical subject/evidence commits.
Broad tools compatibility inheritance was removed from active runtime authority surfaces.

Wave 2A clears the zero-adapter contradiction narrowly: two active live-hashed adapters are now registered and exercised through the frozen Adapter ABI with bounded receipts and deterministic fail-closed artifacts.
KT_PROD_CLEANROOM now has a canonical package manifest, exact hashed lockfile, pytest baseline, and quarantine receipt bundle.

C005 is closed by honest router ratification: the audited static router is now explicitly ratified as canonical current-head control, shadow evidence reproduces the fenced static baseline exactly, fallback evidence is preserved, best-static comparator control remains explicit, and learned routing plus multi-lobe promotion stay blocked until new superiority evidence is earned.

C006 is now prep-ready but not promoted: same-host detached verifier parity, same-host independent clean-environment replay, secret-free outsider verifier tooling, trust-zone boundaries, and the Wave 5 verifier truth surface are all present, while externality remains bounded at E1 until fresh cross-host proof is earned.

Canonical runtime package/import parity is now proven for repo-root, cleanroom-root, and isolated editable-install execution. Stronger wheel/sdist, hermetic, and detached source-independent packaging claims remain unearned.
Package-root and repo-root parity is now validator-backed for the declared canonical runtime lane without overclaiming repo-wide import cleanliness.
TOOLCHAIN_PROVING is now separated from CANONICAL runtime by zone law, runtime-import scanning, and explicit tools-leak checks.

Wave 0.5 now has frozen OrganContract, Adapter ABI, Failure Artifact, Telemetry, Mutation Authority, Challenge Pack, Deletion Law, Benchmark Constitution, Externality Matrix, and Minimum Viable Civilization Run contracts.
Truth barrier v2.1.1 now enforces the Wave 0.5 laws. The only remaining live-validation failure is the dirty-sensitive broad current-worktree suite, which sits outside the narrow Wave 0.5 gate.
Wave 1 now binds declared Sigstore/Rekor, in-toto/SLSA, TUF, and determinism surfaces to exact KT targets in a machine-readable matrix without widening runtime or externality claims.
Wave 1 adds structured telemetry to declared canonical runtime and toolchain proving paths and validates the emitted envelopes instead of treating logging as decorative.
Provider failure behavior is now bounded by timeout, retry, backoff, circuit-breaker, rate-limit, and quota primitives with fail-closed outcomes on the declared provider seam.
Wave 1 now fixes the local/CI reproducibility stance to a narrow, hash-locked strategy without introducing Bazel, new-language bootstrap, or active Nix sprawl.

Successful authenticated live-provider execution and same-host resilience are proven for the canonical LIVE_HASHED OpenAI/OpenRouter lane with signed receipts. This does not raise externality above E1 or prove broader provider coverage.

The exact carriage ceiling was the generic 256-char runtime-context short-string rule being re-applied to envelope.input despite that field already having its own bounded byte ceiling. C017 remediation now preserves the general short-string law, proves full bounded organ payload carriage through core.spine.run, and keeps oversize payloads fail-closed with deterministic artifacts.
